name: United States (1896–1908)
description: Thirteen alternating red and white horizontal stripes (seven red, six white). In the upper hoist, a blue canton bearing 45 white five-pointed stars in six rows of 8-7-8-7-8-7.
history: Adopted on 4 July 1896 following the admission of Utah as the 45th state on 4 January 1896. The 45-star flag served as the official flag of the United States for nearly 12 years, the longest-serving star arrangement of the late 19th century. It flew during the Spanish–American War (1898), the annexation of Hawaii (1898), the acquisition of Puerto Rico, Guam and the Philippines (1898), and the construction of the Panama Canal Zone. It was replaced on 4 July 1908 after the admission of Oklahoma as the 46th state on 16 November 1907.

features.symbolism: the thirteen original British colonies that declared independence in 1776
the union of the states; a new constellation
the 45 states of the Union as of 4 July 1896
